mnemonics.title: Array Index Starting Point
mnemonics.content: ARRAYS ALWAYS START AT ZERO - A S Z (remember 'A' for Array, 'S' for Start, 'Z' for Zero)

mnemonics.title: Row Major Order Formula
mnemonics.content: ROW MAJOR: i × COLS + j - Think 'RICJ' = Row Index × Columns + Column Index

mnemonics.title: Time Complexities
mnemonics.content: ACCESS IS ALWAYS ONE (O(1)) - SEARCH SHIFTS (O(n)) - SORT SHOWS QUICKLY (O(n log n))

acronyms.expansion: Last In First Out - not directly for arrays but relevant for stack implementations using arrays
First In First Out - used in queue implementations
Compressed Sparse Row - efficient sparse matrix storage format
acronyms.usage: Storing large sparse matrices with minimal memory

acronyms.expansion: Coordinate List - sparse matrix format storing row, column, value triplets

visualTips.visualization: Draw boxes side by side in a row. Each box has a number (index) below it starting from 0. Draw an arrow showing direct access to any box using its index.
For row-major: fill row 1 completely, then row 2, then row 3. For column-major: fill column 1 completely, then column 2, then column 3. Both create the same linear sequence for different orders.
Draw a small box array (size 4). When full, draw a larger box (size 8) with original elements copied and moved. Show how doubling provides amortized O(1) insertion.
Show a large 10×10 grid with mostly zeros (draw empty boxes). Mark non-zero positions with X. Then show a compact 3-column table: row, column, value storing only the X entries.

analogies.concept: Array as a Street of Houses
analogies.analogy: Think of an array as houses on a street where each house has a unique house number (index). You can directly go to house number 5 without visiting houses 1-4, just like random access in arrays.

analogies.concept: Array vs Linked List as a Book vs Doodle
analogies.analogy: An array is like pages in a book - fixed order, contiguous, easy to find any page by number. A linked list is like a scrapbook with pages randomly taped - you must flip through to find what you want.

analogies.concept: Dynamic Array Resizing as a Growing Row
analogies.analogy: Imagine a row of cars in a parking lot (array). When full, you open a new parking lot twice the size, move all cars to the new location, and demolish the old lot. Doubling makes this move infrequent.

analogies.concept: Jagged Array as a Triangular Seating
analogies.analogy: A jagged array is like classroom seating where the first row has 5 seats, second row has 4 seats, third row has 6 seats - different lengths per row, unlike a rectangular classroom with equal seats per row.
